name: Public Safety
cardText: Teams and civilians stay mutually aware in emergencies and mass events — even when networks are overloaded or down.

meta.title: Public Safety
meta.description: Viziblezone for public safety: situational awareness for teams and civilians that keeps working when cellular networks are overloaded or down.

hero.title:
When the network dies,
awareness survives
hero.lede: Disasters, mass events and emergencies are exactly when cellular networks fail. Viziblezone's direct device-to-device architecture was born from the same DMO discipline behind professional mission-critical communications.

scenario.title:
Hour one of
the emergency
paragraphs: An earthquake, a mass-casualty event, a large-scale outage. Cell networks are saturated or dark precisely when responders need to know where people are — and when civilians need to be findable.

Because Viziblezone communicates directly between devices, presence and location awareness keep flowing with no towers at all. Responders see who is near them; trapped or endangered civilians remain announcible to anyone in range.

items.title: Zero-infrastructure operation
items.text: Designed to keep working through total network collapse — the failure mode that breaks every cloud-based solution.

items.title: DMO in its DNA
items.text: The architecture follows DMO principles from professional mission-critical communications, adapted to the devices everyone carries.

items.title: Civilians included
items.text: Protection extends beyond equipped teams to the public — the phones already in every pocket become part of the picture.

items.title: Standing capability
items.text: Nothing to deploy on the day of the event. The capability is already resident on the devices, waiting.

deployment.title:
A resilience layer,
not a gadget
paragraphs: For agencies, Viziblezone integrates into responder devices and command applications. For population-scale resilience, it ships with partner devices or national preparedness apps — dormant in normal times, decisive in the first hour of a crisis.
bullets: Responder mode: team awareness on standard smartphones
Population mode: embedded in partner devices or preparedness apps
Exercises: measurable performance in network-denied drills

cta.title:
Plan for the
worst hour
cta.lede: We work with agencies and partners on network-denied awareness capabilities.
